elements.name: Title
texts: "For nothing in the world would I abandon this journey"

elements.name: Description
texts: A famous account of a 1398 visit by Viscount Ramon de Perellós to Saint Patrick's Purgatory

elements.name: Source
texts: The Journey of Viscount Ramon de Perellós to Saint Patrick's Purgatory, p. 5

elements.name: Contributor
texts: CELT: Corpus of Electronic Texts: a project of University College, Cork. Translated by Alan Mac an Bhaird.

elements.name: Rights
texts: Citation for the purposes of criticism

elements.name: Text
texts: "And after some days I spoke to the pope, telling him that for nothing in the world would I abandon this journey; and having obtained his blessing, I departed from him on the feast of Our Lady in September in the aforesaid year and went my way through France to the king's court in Paris — I was a former servant of his and his father, whose name was Charles, had reared me from a very early age and had likewise made me his chamberlain; and from the king of France and from his uncles, the brothers dukes of Berry and Burgundy, I had letters of recommendation to the king of England (who was his son in law) and other lords of England (on beginning the marriage they had made a truce for 30 years). I left Paris and by daily stages came to Calais where I took ship for England."

elements.name: Original Format
texts: The Journey of Viscount Ramon De Perellós to Saint Patrick's Purgatory. Alan Mac an Bhaird (ed), First edition [i + 23 pp] CELT Cork (2012)
